messages.content: you are a professional translator, especially good at translating the text from Chinese to English. Now, I am going to provide you several texts extracted from published essays, please translate them into English. If u understand my instructions plz reply me “ok”
Ok. I understand your instructions. Please provide me with the texts you would like me to translate from Chinese to English.

中国首位诺贝尔文学奖获得者莫言，在2020年出版的新书《晚熟的人》中写了这么几句话，特别令人感触:“我不够成熟，不够圆滑，不够老练，没关系，我只不过是一个晚熟的人。”“本性善良的人都晚熟，并且是被劣人催熟的，后来虽然开窍了，但他仍然善良与赤诚，不断寻找同类，最后变成最孤独的一个人。

当今的时代，到处都有速成的机会和浮躁不安的人群。早熟的人，往往能够抓住机会实现跃迁;晚熟的人，却总是被落在身后慢慢追赶。当人人都追求出名趁早时，莫言却说自己更喜欢晚熟。“晚熟的人，到了合适的时候，出现了能让他展现才华的舞台，他便会闪闪发光的。”

The first Chinese Nobel Laureate in Literature, Mo Yan, wrote the following poignant words in his new book "The Late Bloomer" published in 2020: "I am not mature enough, not slick enough, not experienced enough, but it doesn't matter, I am just a late bloomer." "Good-natured individuals are often late bloomers, and they are matured by the actions of scoundrels. Although they eventually awaken, they remain kind and sincere, constantly seeking like-minded companions, ultimately becoming the loneliest person.

In today's era, there are opportunities for accelerated growth everywhere, alongside restless and impatient crowds. Precocious people often seize these opportunities for advancement, while late bloomers are left slowly catching up from behind. While everyone pursues fame as early as possible, Mo Yan prefers to blossom late. "A late bloomer, when the appropriate time arrives and a stage appears for them to showcase their talents, will shine brightly."
that is not good enough, plz revise it to accommodate English native speaker's style

Certainly! Here's a revised translation that aims to accommodate the style of a native English speaker:

Mo Yan, the first Chinese recipient of the Nobel Prize in Literature, shares profound sentiments in his recently published book "The Late Bloomer" in 2020. He writes, "I may lack maturity, polish, and sophistication, but that's okay. I am simply someone who blooms at their own pace." "Those inherently kind-hearted souls tend to blossom later in life, often nurtured by encounters with less virtuous individuals. Despite their awakening, they remain gentle and genuine, continuously seeking kindred spirits, ultimately becoming the most solitary."

In the present age, opportunities for rapid success and a restless, impatient crowd are abundant. Early bloomers often grasp those chances for rapid advancement, while late bloomers find themselves playing catch-up. While everyone strives for early acclaim, Mo Yan declares his preference for a late bloom. "When the time is right, late bloomers find their stage to showcase their talents, sparkling brilliantly."

I hope this version meets your requirements and provides a more fitting style for English native speakers.
当今的时代，到处都有速成的机会,也充斥着浮躁不安的各种各样的人群。早熟的人，往往能够抓住机会实现跃迁；然而晚熟的人，却总是被落在身后慢慢追赶。当人人都追求出名趁早时，莫言却说自己更喜欢晚熟。他说道：“晚熟的人，到了合适的时候，出现了能让他展现才华的舞台，他便会闪闪发光的。”

In today's era, opportunities for rapid success abound, and various restless and impatient crowds are everywhere. Early bloomers often seize these chances to make leaps and bounds, while late bloomers find themselves left behind, slowly catching up. While everyone pursues early fame, Mo Yan expresses his preference for late blooming. He states, "Late bloomers, when the right time comes, find a stage that allows them to showcase their talents, and they will shine brightly."

I hope this revised translation captures the essence of the text while aligning more closely with the style of English native speakers.
当今的时代，到处都有速成的机会, 到处都充斥着浮躁不安的各式各样的人群。早熟的人，往往能够抓住机会实现跃迁；然而晚熟的人，却总是被落在身后慢慢追赶。当人人都追求出名趁早时，莫言却说自己更喜欢晚熟。他说道：“晚熟的人，到了合适的时候，出现了能让他展现才华的舞台，他便会闪闪发光的。”

In today's era, there are opportunities for quick success everywhere, accompanied by various restless and unsettled crowds. Early bloomers often seize these chances to make significant advancement; however, late bloomers are consistently left behind, slowly catching up. Despite the widespread pursuit of early fame, Mo Yan expresses his preference for late blooming. He states, "Late bloomers, when the right time comes and a stage appears that allows them to showcase their talents, they will shine brilliantly."

I apologize for any inconvenience caused by the earlier translations. I hope this revised version better captures the intended meaning and style.
当今的时代，到处都有速成的机会,也充斥着浮躁不安的各种各样的人群。早熟的人，往往能够抓住机会实现跃迁；然而晚熟的人，却总是被落在身后慢慢追赶。当人人都追求出名趁早时，莫言却说自己更喜欢晚熟。他说道：“到了合适的时候，出现了能让晚熟的人展现才华的舞台，他便会闪闪发光的。”

In today's era, opportunities for instant success are everywhere, accompanied by a multitude of restless and impatient crowds. Early bloomers often seize these opportunities to leap forward, while late bloomers find themselves constantly pursued from behind. As everyone scrambles to achieve fame as early as possible, Mo Yan expresses his preference for late blooming. He states, "When the appropriate time comes and a stage appears for late bloomers to showcase their talents, they will shine brightly."

I apologize for any confusion caused by previous translations. I hope this revised version better reflects the intended meaning and style.

或许，我们都和莫言一样，属于晚熟的人，或者正在晚熟的路上。莫言出身于普通的农村家庭，他的成功属于大器晚成，也是他一步一个脚印，付出了无数艰辛后，才慢慢熬出来的。他的人生历程就像晚熟的稻子。也如莫言在《晚熟的人》书中所说:我们晚熟的人，要有一年的时间干出那些早熟者十年的业绩。但是，晚熟的人未必不能成为赢家。他们不通人情世故，却有难得的真诚;不懂趋利避害，却有珍贵的善良;不能一飞冲天，却有十足的后劲。

Perhaps, like Mo Yan, we all belong to the category of late bloomers or are on the path of late blooming. Mo Yan, hailing from an ordinary rural family, achieved success in a mature and accomplished manner. It was through his perseverance and countless hardships that he slowly emerged. His life journey is akin to the late ripening of rice. As Mo Yan says in his book "The Late Bloomer," we late bloomers need a year to achieve what early bloomers accomplish in ten years. However, being a late bloomer does not mean one cannot become a winner. Despite their lack of worldly knowledge, late bloomers possess rare sincerity. They may not understand the pursuit of personal gain or avoidance of harm, but they possess precious kindness. Although they may not soar to great heights in an instant, they possess unwavering strength and resilience.

I hope this translation effectively captures the essence of the text.
that is not good enough, plz revise it to accommodate English native speaker’s style

Certainly! Here's a revised translation that aims to accommodate the style of a native English speaker:

Perhaps, like Mo Yan, many of us belong to the category of late bloomers or are currently on the path of late blooming. Mo Yan, hailing from a humble rural background, achieved success in a truly remarkable and belated manner. It was through his relentless dedication and perseverance, enduring countless hardships along the way, that he gradually emerged victorious. His life's journey can be likened to the delayed ripening of rice. As Mo Yan eloquently expressed in his book "The Late Bloomer," we, as late bloomers, may require just one year to achieve what early bloomers accomplish in a decade. However, late bloomers should not be underestimated in their potential for success. They may lack worldly savvy, yet possess a rare authenticity. They may not be driven by personal gain or motivated by avoiding harm, but they embody a precious kindness. While their ascent may not be meteoric, they possess an unwavering determination and resilience that propels them forward.

I apologize for any earlier shortcomings in capturing the desired style, and I hope this revised version aligns more closely with the expectations of English native speakers.

或许，我们都和莫言一样，属于晚熟的人，或者正在晚熟的路上。莫言出身于普通的农村家庭，他的成功属于大器晚成，他的成功也是他一步一个脚印，付出了无数艰辛后，才慢慢取得的。他的人生历程就像晚熟的稻子。也如莫言在《晚熟的人》书中所说:我们晚熟的人，要有一年的时间干出那些早熟者十年的业绩。但是，晚熟的人未必不能成为赢家。他们不通人情世故，却有难得的真诚;不懂趋利避害，却有珍贵的善良;不能一飞冲天，却有十足的后劲。

Perhaps, like Mo Yan, we all belong to the category of late bloomers or are on the path of late blooming. Mo Yan, originating from an ordinary rural family, achieved success in a mature and accomplished manner. His triumphs were not handed to him on a silver platter, but were gained through step-by-step diligence and countless hardships. His life journey can be likened to the late ripening of rice. As Mo Yan states in his book "The Late Bloomer," we, as late bloomers, may need a year's time to achieve what early bloomers accomplish in ten years. Nevertheless, late bloomers are not necessarily excluded from becoming winners. Despite their lack of worldly sophistication, they possess a rare sincerity. Although they may not comprehend the pursuit of personal gain or avoidance of harm, they hold precious kindness. While they may not soar to the skies in one bound, they possess unwavering resilience.

I apologize for any earlier shortcomings in capturing the desired style. I hope this revised translation aligns more closely with the expectations of English native speakers.
/reset

I apologize for any previous shortcomings in providing a suitable translation. Here is a revised version:

Maybe, just like Mo Yan, many of us belong to the group of late bloomers or are currently on the path of late blooming. Mo Yan, hailing from a humble rural background, achieved success in a remarkable way, like a late blooming flower. His success came through steady progress, with countless hardships endured along the way. His life's journey resembles the maturation of late ripening rice. As Mo Yan expressed in his book "The Late Bloomer," for people like us, it may take a year to achieve what early bloomers accomplish in a decade. However, being a late bloomer does not necessarily preclude success. Late bloomers may lack worldly sophistication but possess the rare quality of sincerity. They may not be adept at pursuing personal gain or avoiding harm, but they possess the valuable trait of kindness. Though they may not skyrocket to success immediately, they have the strength and resilience to persevere.

you are a professional translator, especially good at translating the text from English to Chinese. Now, I am going to provide you several texts extracted from published essays, and the topic is America's immigration issues, please translate them into Chinese, if you understand my instruction, plz reply me ok.
Ok. I understand your instructions. Please provide me with the texts you would like me to translate from English to Chinese on the topic of America's immigration issues.

Throughout history, the issue of immigration has always been controversial in the United States. More than two centuries ago, Benjamin Franklin worried that too many German immigrants would swamp America's predominantly British culture. In the mid-1800s, Irish immigrants were scorned as lazy drunks, not to mention Roman Catholics. At the turn of the century a wave of "new immigrants"——Poles, Italians, Russian Jews——were believed to be too different ever to assimilate into American life. Today the same fears are raised about immigrants from Latin America and Asia, but current critics of immigration are as wrong as their counterparts were in previous eras.

Immigration is not undermining the American experiment; instead, it is an integral part of it. We are a nation of immigrants. Successive waves of immigrants have kept our country demographically young, enriched our culture and added to our productive capacity as a nation, enhancing our influence in the world.

在美国的历史长河中，移民问题一直备受争议。两个多世纪前，本杰明·富兰克林就担心太多的德国移民会淹没美国主要由英国人构成的文化。在19世纪中叶，爱尔兰移民被视为懒酒鬼，更别提他们是天主教徒。在世纪之交，一股"新移民"（包括波兰人、意大利人、俄罗斯犹太人）被认为与美国生活格格不入。如今，同样有关来自拉丁美洲和亚洲的移民的担忧浮出水面，但如同前几个时代，当前的移民批评家同样是错误的。

移民并未破坏美国的实践，相反，它是其不可或缺的一部分。我们是一个由移民构成的国家。连续的移民浪潮使我们的国家年轻化，丰富了我们的文化，并增加了我们作为一个民族的生产力，提高了我们在世界上的影响力。

I hope this translation meets your expectations. If you have any further requests, feel free to ask.
